title: Wasnt Asuka dead already
wikitext:
[]: Wasn't Asuka dead already?             Quote Post by Coldwire » Fri Nov 16, 2012 6:23 pm Why does Shinji go to Asuka for help during Pre-instrumentality? I mean, I understand that he is scared of Misato and Rei and he has no one else to turn to, but wasn't Asuka dead for like ten minutes. My point is, what would have happened if she said: ¨Ok Shinji, I love you too, do not iniciate third impact¨, when Shinji declares in the kitchen. Would she come back to life to be with him, is that even possible without instrumentality? I can imagine Shinji saying:¨Soooo, you love me, but you're dead... Goddamit I should have thought this through¨.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 16, 2012 6:37 pm This is a twisted replay of the added scene at the start of episode 24'; just like the previous replay of the kiss scene from episode 15, with the kiss becoming a kick. At this stage in the proceedings, being dead isn't much of a limitation, as souls are all being gathered together -- as we saw before both the replays Asuka's soul has been joined in complementation with Shinji [center] [/center] and then they, and others, listen in on Misato's memories of her week abed with Kaji (another case where being dead is not being an impediment). If Shinji had been talked down from his hissy fit -- well maybe we might have gotten a "Congratulations!" ending, and not a "Kimochi warui." one.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Nov 17, 2012 11:07 am At that point in time, Shinji probably wasn't thinking much about life and death in concrete terms. Certainly, Rei/Lilith could've brought Asuka back to life (we know this because she facilitates people returning, Asuka among them anyway) had Shinji decided not to pull the trigger on third impact, and she probably would've allowed the recently-dead to come back had they been so-inclined.

[]:             Quote Post by Lord ikari shinji » Tue Sep 24, 2013 4:53 pm hmmmmmm.... good question but was she really ever killed? I mean yea her eva was destroy/ fucking devoured but was she die hmmmmm. imo I believe she survived due to Rei savin her {for some reason maybe because of Shinji's heart or feelings of depair due to him failing to save her } by obtain her soul and body/ that would explain her having bangles as apose to her not. As for if she{Asuka} said "I love you" Shinji/Asuka both may have return sooner, Shinji wouldn't have felt abandoned for the first time in his terrible life, and would have man-ed up and pull the plug on third impact sooner

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Sep 24, 2013 4:59 pm Her soul was lingering, it hadn't been reincarnated yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Sep 24, 2013 5:03 pm Jeez, it's been forever since I made a post in Discussion! Lord ikari Shinji wrote: hmmmmmm.... good question but was she really ever killed?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 24, 2013 5:06 pm Lord ikari Shinji wrote: hmmmmmm.... good question but was she really ever killed?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Sep 24, 2013 8:05 pm Note that this is before Shinji gives the order to tangify everyone, but after unit one turns into the "origin and destination of all souls" if I remember Fuyu's quote correctly. The ppl he's blending/communicating with at this point are ONLY the dead ones: Misato, Ritsuko, Asuka... You can presume that those ghostly Rei's already collected their souls, or that it has to do with the Lol, unit one and whatever the fusion of the two means/really is.

[]:             Quote Post by Coldwire » Wed Sep 25, 2013 3:53 am In my opinion Asuka was killed, no doubt about that. I also think it is really her the one that Shinji is talking to throughout instrumentality(as it is really Misato, Ritsuko or anybody else for that matter). My point was that even though Rei had been gathering souls already, instrumentality hasn't really taken place yet, the souls are not unified on the sea of lcl. Was it possible for Asuka to comeback if Shinji had rejected the HIP right there on the kitchen? I think not.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Sep 25, 2013 4:08 am Is there evidence to reject that Asuka might have been fatally wounded, definitely going to die, in the process of bleeding to death but NOT COMPLETELY dead yet (i.e. no brain and no heart activity)? She's one stubborn and temperamental girl, we know that for a fact...

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Sep 25, 2013 4:59 am Well, we definitely saw her lose an eye and had her arm cut in half. If the pain of that didn't kill her, I doubt she would have survived the harpies the entire Eva. Additionally, during the scene when the harpies are tearing off Eva-02's armor and eating its guts, we can see Asuka squirming in pain and holding her stomach inside the Eva, which made me wonder if she was also losing corresponding internal organs due to her high synch rate... Even if all of that didn't kill her, then she probably wouldn't have survived the N2 Mine. We know it's not impossible to see a dead person in Instrumentality because Misato appears several times throughout the Instrumentality scenes, despite having been fatally shot and conclusively blown up earlier.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Sep 25, 2013 5:47 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Additionally, during the scene when the harpies are tearing off Eva-02's armor and eating its guts, we can see Asuka squirming in pain and holding her stomach inside the Eva

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Sep 25, 2013 5:53 am Asuka squirming happens just before the arm-split.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Sep 25, 2013 8:25 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: Is there evidence to reject that Asuka might have been fatally wounded, definitely going to die, in the process of bleeding to death but NOT COMPLETELY dead yet (i.e. no brain and no heart activity)? She's one stubborn and temperamental girl, we know that for a fact...

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Sep 25, 2013 8:51 am Bagheera wrote: The final false lance thrown by the MPEs pierced Unit 02's core, and with it the entry plug. She was dead and gone, no question about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Sep 25, 2013 9:06 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: Hitting an Entry Plug is no guarantee of killing a pilot. Toji's Entry Plug was also crushed badly, yet he didn't die. Same for Asuka in Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Sep 25, 2013 9:45 am Bagheera wrote: In both those cases the entry plug wasn't hit by an Eva-sized weapon. Having the core pierced when the pilot's synced with the Eva means the pilot was hit directly, which would kind of have to kill them.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Sep 25, 2013 9:51 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: I disagree. Direct damage to the Entry Plug may not always equal instant death of the pilot, a bit like not every car accident has lethal consequences and people have been known nto to die on the spot even when the vehicle was badly wrecked?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Sep 25, 2013 10:58 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: I disagree. Direct damage to the Entry Plug may not always equal instant death of the pilot, a bit like not every car accident has lethal consequences and people have been known nto to die on the spot even when the vehicle was badly wrecked?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Sep 25, 2013 11:02 am The entry plug is irrelevant. Her high synch ratio meant the damage to the Eva itself was physically tearing her apart. She was in pieces by the time the harpies were done with her, even if they never touched the plug itself.
summary: []: Wasn't Asuka dead already?             Quote Post by Coldwire » Fri Nov 16, 2012 6:23 pm Why does Shinji go to Asuka for help during Pre-instrumentality? I mean, I understand that he is scared of Misato and Rei and he has no one else to turn to, but wasn't Asuka dead for like ten minutes. My point is, what would have happened if she said: ¨Ok Shinji, I love you too, do not iniciate third impact¨, when Shinji declares in the kitchen.
